questions.text: If a dielectric material is placed between two plates of a capacitor, the net capacitance of the capacitor:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: When a dielectric material is placed between two plates of a capacitor, the presence of the dielectric reduces the electric field between the plates. This is because the polarized molecules in the dielectric material align themselves with the electric field, weakening it. As a result, the capacitance of the capacitor increases. Therefore, option (a) is not correct.

○The strength of the electric field is reduced due to the presence of dielectric. If the total charge on the plates is kept constant, then the potential difference is reduced across the capacitor plates. In this way, dielectric increases the capacitance of the capacitor.So, option A,C,D is wrong .

If a dielectric material is placed between the plates of a capacitor and the separation distance between the plates remains the same, the capacitance will increase due to the dielectric constant of the material. Therefore, the net capacitance of the capacitor does not remain constant. Hence, option (c) is not correct.

If the dielectric material completely fills the space between the plates of a capacitor, the electric field between the plates is essentially eliminated. In this case, the capacitance of the capacitor becomes zero because there is no potential difference between the plates. However, in most practical scenarios, the dielectric material is not in direct contact with the plates but is inserted between them. Therefore, the option (d) is not correct

questions.explanation: ○The strength of the electric field is reduced due to the presence of dielectric. If the total charge on the plates is kept constant, then the potential difference is reduced across the capacitor plates. In this way, dielectric increases the capacitance of the capacitor.So, option A,C,D is wrong .
hints: dielectric material is placed between two plates of a capacitor

questions.text: In what form is the energy stored in a charged capacitor?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Energy stored in a charged capacitor is not in the form of gravitational energy. Gravitational energy is associated with the gravitational field and the position of objects relative to each other in a gravitational field, whereas a capacitor stores energy in an electric field.

This option is partially correct. The energy stored in a charged capacitor is indeed related to electrical potential. When a capacitor is charged, work is done to move charges from one plate to the other, creating a potential difference between the plates. This potential difference, also known as voltage, represents stored electrical energy in the capacitor.

Energy stored in a charged capacitor is not in the form of a magnetic field. Capacitors primarily store energy in an electric field between their plates. While there might be small induced magnetic fields around the conductors carrying current during charging and discharging processes, the predominant energy storage mechanism in a capacitor is electric, not

This option is somewhat related to option b). The energy stored in a capacitor is indeed related to potential, specifically electrical potential or voltage. The potential difference between the plates of the capacitor, caused by the accumulation of charges on the plates, represents the stored energy.

questions.explanation: Energy is stored in a form of electrostatic field between plates of capacitor. This energy can be expressed in the form of work done by the battery.
hints: form is the energy stored in a charged capacitor

questions.text: Three times decrease in the distance between the plates of a parallel plate, capacitor will _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect. Capacitance is inversely proportional to the distance between the plates. Reducing the distance increases capacitance.
Incorrect. This is a misunderstanding of the relationship between capacitance and distance. Capacitance does not decrease when plate distance is reduced.
Correct. Since capacitance (C) is inversely proportional to the distance (d) between the plates, reducing the distance to one-third triples the capacitance.
Incorrect. The increase in capacitance is directly proportional to the reduction in distance, which in this case is three times, not six.

questions.explanation: Capacitance of a parallel plate capacitor is given by the formula C = ε₀(A/d), where C is the capacitance, ε₀ is the permittivity of free space, A is the area of the plates, and d is the distance between the plates. When the distance d is decreased to one-third, the capacitance C increases by a factor of three. Therefore, a three times decrease in the distance results in a threefold increase in capacitance. Other options are incorrect as they either misinterpret or miscalculate the relationship between capacitance and distance.

questions.text: Charged body attracts uncharged body by _ induction.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Charged body attracts an uncharged body by electrostatic induction.Electrostatic induction is a process in which a charged object (the charged body) causes a redistribution of charges in a nearby uncharged object (the uncharged body) without direct contact. When a charged object is brought close to the uncharged object, the charges in the uncharged object are attracted or repelled by the charged object, causing a temporary separation of charges within the uncharged object.For example, if a positively charged object is brought close to an uncharged object, the electrons in the uncharged object are attracted towards the positively charged object, causing a slight shift of negative charge towards the side closest to the charged object. At the same time, the positive charges in the uncharged object are repelled, resulting in a slight shift of positive charge towards the side farthest from the charged object.As a result of this charge redistribution, the side of the uncharged object closest to the charged object becomes negatively charged, while the side farthest from the charged object becomes positively charged. This creates an attractive force between the charged and uncharged objects, causing the uncharged object to move towards the charged object.It's important to note that electrostatic induction is a temporary effect. Once the charged object is removed, the charges in the uncharged object will redistribute back to their original positions, and the uncharged object will return to its neutral state.

Charged body attracts an uncharged body by electrostatic induction.Electrostatic induction is a process in which a charged object (the charged body) causes a redistribution of charges in a nearby uncharged object (the uncharged body) without direct contact. When a charged object is brought close to the uncharged object, the charges in the uncharged object are attracted or repelled by the charged object, causing a temporary separation of charges within the uncharged object.For example, if a positively charged object is brought close to an uncharged object, the electrons in the uncharged object are attracted towards the positively charged object, causing a slight shift of negative charge towards the side closest to the charged object. At the same time, the positive charges in the uncharged object are repelled, resulting in a slight shift of positive charge towards the side farthest from the charged object.As a result of this charge redistribution, the side of the uncharged object closest to the charged object becomes negatively charged, while the side farthest from the charged object becomes positively charged. This creates an attractive force between the charged and uncharged objects, causing the uncharged object to move towards the charged object.It's important to note that electrostatic induction is a temporary effect. Once the charged object is removed, the charges in the uncharged object will redistribute back to their original positions, and the uncharged object will return to its neutral state.
Incorrect as only option A is correct.
Incorrect as option A is correct.

questions.explanation: 'Electrostatic induction', also known as "electrostatic influence" or simply "influence", is a redistribution of electric charge in an object, caused by the influence of nearby charges.

questions.text: You have three capacitors, each of 2 µC. In which of the following combinations of the three capacitors, the resultant capacitance is 3µC?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Two capacitors are in series, one in parallel: Let's say C_a and C_b are the two capacitors in series, and C_c is the capacitor in parallel. The total capacitance (C_total) in this combination is given by:1 / C_total = 1 / (C_a + C_b) +C_cSubstituting the values:1 / C_total = (1 /2 µC + 1/2µC) + 2 µC= 4/4 µC + 2 µC= 3 µCC_total = 3µCThe total capacitance when two capacitors are in series and one in parallel is 3 µCso this option is not correct.

options.text: Two capacitors are in series, one in parallel
options.explanation: b) Two capacitors are in series, one in parallel: Let's say C_a and C_b are the two capacitors in series, and C_c is the capacitor in parallel. The total capacitance (C_total) in this combination is given by:1 / C_total = 1 / (C_a + C_b) +C_cSubstituting the values:1 / C_total = (1 /2 µC + 1/2µC) + 2 µC= 4/4 µC + 2 µC= 3 µCC_total = 3µCThe total capacitance when two capacitors are in series and one in parallel is 3 µCso this option is not correct.

The total capacitance (C_total) in this combination is given by:1 / C_total = 1 / (C_a + C_b) +C_cSubstituting the values:1 / C_total = (1 /2 µC + 1/2µC) + 2 µC= 4/4 µC + 2 µC= 3 µCC_total = 3µCThe total capacitance when two capacitors are in series and one in parallel is 3 µCso this option is not correct.

questions.explanation: b) Two capacitors are in series, one in parallel: Let's say C_a and C_b are the two capacitors in series, and C_c is the capacitor in parallel. The total capacitance (C_total) in this combination is given by:1 / C_total = 1 / (C_a + C_b) +C_cSubstituting the values:1 / C_total = (1 /2 µC + 1/2µC) + 2 µC= 4/4 µC + 2 µC= 3 µCC_total = 3µC The total capacitance when two capacitors are in series and one in parallel is 3 µC so this option is correct.

questions.text: Farad is defined as?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Newton/volt doesn't define any quantity

A farad (F) is the standard unit of capacitance (C) in the International System of Units (SI). It indicates the ability of a substance to hold an electric charge. The value of most electrical capacitors is expressed in farads, microfarads (µF) or nanofarads (nF).The SI unit of electrical capacitance, equal to the capacitance of a capacitor in which one coulomb of charge causes a potential difference of one volt.Farad = Coulomb / volt
Coulomb/joule doesn't define any quantity.However , 1 joule per coulomb = 1 volt = 1 V.
Coulomb/newton doesn't define any quantityE = F/q So, the unit of the electric field is Newton/Coulomb

The value of most electrical capacitors is expressed in farads, microfarads (µF) or nanofarads (nF).The SI unit of electrical capacitance, equal to the capacitance of a capacitor in which one coulomb of charge causes a potential difference of one volt. Farad = Coulomb / volt

This results in the same potential difference across each capacitor. The total capacitance of the combination is the sum of the individual capacitances.In the given expression, since all the capacitors are added together, it represents a parallel combination.Option B: In a series combination of capacitors, the positive terminal of one capacitor is connected to the negative terminal of the next capacitor. questions.text: A capacitor can store its energy in its:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A capacitor does not store energy in its magnetic field. Capacitors are primarily designed to store and release electrical energy through the accumulation and separation of charges on their plates, utilizing the electric field. Magnetic fields are associated with inductors, not capacitors.

This option is partially correct. Capacitors typically consist of two parallel conducting plates separated by a dielectric material. The energy is stored in the electric field created between these plates when the capacitor is charged. So, while the parallel plates play a role in the energy storage process, the actual energy is stored in the electric field.

A capacitor can store its energy in its electric field.When a capacitor is charged, it accumulates electric potential energy in the form of an electric field between its plates. The energy is stored in the electric field as a result of the separation of positive and negative charges on the capacitor plates. The energy is released when the capacitor discharges, and the charges flow from one plate to the other, resulting in a flow of electric current.

A coil, also known as an inductor, stores energy in its magnetic field. Inductors store energy in the form of a magnetic field when a current passes through them. While inductors and capacitors are both components used in electronic circuits, they store energy in different ways. Capacitors store energy in the electric field, while inductors store energy in the magnetic field.A capacitor can store its energy in its electric field.

questions.explanation: Option A: A capacitor does not store energy in its magnetic field. Capacitors are primarily designed to store and release electrical energy through the accumulation and separation of charges on their plates, utilizing the electric field. Magnetic fields are associated with inductors, not capacitors.Option B: This option is partially correct. Capacitors typically consist of two parallel conducting plates separated by a dielectric material. The energy is stored in the electric field created between these plates when the capacitor is charged. So, while the parallel plates play a role in the energy storage process, the actual energy is stored in the electric field.Option C: A capacitor can store its energy in its electric field. When a capacitor is charged, it accumulates electric potential energy in the form of an electric field between its plates. The energy is stored in the electric field as a result of the separation of positive and negative charges on the capacitor plates. The energy is released when the capacitor discharges, and the charges flow from one plate to the other, resulting in a flow of electric current.Option D: A coil, also known as an inductor, stores energy in its magnetic field. Inductors store energy in the form of a magnetic field when a current passes through them. While inductors and capacitors are both components used in electronic circuits, they store energy in different ways. Capacitors store energy in the electric field, while inductors store energy in the magnetic field.A capacitor can store its energy in its electric field.

questions.text: The capacitance of a capacitor is a measure of its ability to:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The capacitance of a capacitor is a measure of its ability to store electric charge. Capacitance is defined as the ratio of the magnitude of the electric charge stored on one plate of a capacitor to the potential difference (voltage) across the plates. It is denoted by the symbol C and is measured in farads (F).
A capacitor does not store electric field. It creates an electric field between its plates when it is charged.
A capacitor does not gain potential difference. It stores charge, which creates a potential difference between its plates.
A capacitor does not store magnetic field. It is an electrical device, not a magnetic device.

questions.explanation: The capacitance of a capacitor is a measure of its ability to store charge. The greater the capacitance, the more charge the capacitor can store.The capacitance of a capacitor is defined as the ratio of the charge stored on the capacitor to the voltage difference across the capacitor. The formula for capacitance is:C = Q / V where:C is the capacitance in farads (F)Q is the charge in coulombs (C)V is the voltage difference in volts (V)A capacitor stores charge by creating an electric field between its plates. The electric field is created by the difference in charge between the plates. The greater the difference in charge, the stronger the electric field.The capacitance of a capacitor is affected by the following factors:The area of the plates: The greater the area of the plates, the greater the capacitance.The distance between the plates: The closer the plates, the greater the capacitance.The dielectric material between the plates: The dielectric material is a material that reduces the electric field between the plates. The greater the dielectric constant of the material, the greater the capacitance.The capacitance of a capacitor does not affect its ability to store electric field or gain potential difference.

options.text: To deposit 63% of the equilibrium charge

options.text: To deposit 36% of the equilibrium charge

options.text: To deposit 63 times of the equilibrium charge
options.explanation: Incorrect as per formulaTo deposit 63 times of the equilibrium charge: The time constant refers to a percentage of the equilibrium charge, not a multiple of it.

options.text: To deposit 36 times of the equilibrium charge
options.explanation: Incorrect as per formulaTo deposit 36 times of the equilibrium charge: This is also incorrect for the same reason as the previous option. The time constant is related to a percentage, not a multiple.

questions.text: For a parallel plate capacitor, the energy density is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option represents half of the product of the relative permittivity (εᵣ), vacuum permittivity (ε₀), and the magnitude of the electric field (E). However, the correct expression for energy density in a parallel plate capacitor involves the square of the electric field (E²), not just E. Therefore, option A is not the correct choice for the energy density of a parallel plate capacitor.

U/V= ½ ε. εr E2 ﻿This option correctly represents the energy density (u) for a parallel plate capacitor. It considers the square of the magnitude of the electric field (E²), which is consistent with the correct formula for energy density. Additionally, it accounts for the relative permittivity (εᵣ) and vacuum permittivity (ε₀). Therefore, option B is the correct choice.

This option only includes the relative permittivity (εᵣ), vacuum permittivity (ε₀), and the magnitude of the electric field (E), but it lacks the necessary factor of ½ and the square of the electric field (E²) required for the correct expression of energy density. Therefore, option C is not the correct choice.

This option includes the relative permittivity (εᵣ), vacuum permittivity (ε₀), and the square of the magnitude of the electric field (E²). However, it is missing the necessary factor of ½ in front of the expression. The correct formula for energy density in a parallel plate capacitor includes this factor of ½. Therefore, option D is not the correct choice.

questions.explanation: Energy density is the total energy per unit volume of the capacitor, as the electrostatic energy stored in a parallel plate capacitor is U=½ CV2for a parallel plate capacitor, C=(ε0εrA)/d and V = Ed; so,U=½ (ε0εrA/d)(Ed)2=½ ε0εrE2(Ad),Where Ad = Volume of the capacitor (V). ThenEnergy density U/V = ½ ε0εrE2.

questions.text: One coulomb charge is carried by:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: One Coulomb of charge is equivalent to the charge possessed by approximately 6.25 × 1018 electrons. A Coulomb is the SI unit of electric charge, representing a large amount of charge. It is defined as the charge that flows through a conductor when one Ampere (1 A) of current passes through it in one second.

This option is not correct. As mentioned above, one electron carries a charge of approximately 1.6 x 10^-19 coulombs. To make up one coulomb of charge, we would need many electrons (about 6.25 x 10^18 electrons).

This option is also not correct. Protons are positively charged particles, and each proton carries a fundamental unit of positive charge, which is also approximately 1.6 x 10^-19 coulombs. To make up one coulomb of charge, we would need many protons (about 6.25 x 10^18 protons).
This option is not correct. As mentioned earlier, one coulomb of charge is equivalent to the charge carried by about 6.25 x 10^18 electrons, not 1.6 x 10^19 electrons.

questions.explanation: Remember that 1 coulomb charge is carried by 6.25 × 1018 electrons.

questions.text: What is a capacitor?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: A device that produces audio sounds
options.explanation: This option describes a speaker or a transducer, which converts electrical signals into sound waves. It does not accurately describe a capacitor.

options.text: A device that dissipates electric charge
options.explanation: This option describes a resistor or a device that converts electrical energy into heat or other forms of energy. It does not accurately describe a capacitor.

options.text: A device that stores electric charge in an electric field
options.explanation: A capacitor is an electronic component used in electrical circuits to store and release electrical energy. It consists of two conductive plates separated by an insulating material called a dielectric. When a voltage difference (potential difference) is applied across the plates, electric charge accumulates on the plates, creating an electric field between them. This stored electric charge in the electric field creates potential energy, which can be released when needed. Capacitors are commonly used in various electronic devices and circuits for energy storage, filtering, timing, and coupling applications.

options.text: A device that emits radio signals
options.explanation: This option describes a radio transmitter or an antenna, which emits electromagnetic waves for communication purposes. It does not accurately describe a capacitor.

questions.explanation: A capacitor is a device that stores electric charges in an electric field. A capacitor is an electrical component that stores potential energy. Capacitors hold positive and negative energy on two separate plates separated by an insulator. A capacitor(s) is called cap(s) for short.

questions.text: What effect does a dielectric have on the capacitance of a capacitor?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: When a dielectric material is inserted between the plates of a capacitor, it increases the capacitance of the capacitor. The extent of this increase is determined by the dielectric constant (k) or relative permittivity of the material. The dielectric constant represents the ratio of the capacitance of the capacitor with the dielectric material to the capacitance of the same capacitor without the dielectric material. Mathematically, the capacitance with the dielectric (C' ) is given by C'=k×C, where C is the capacitance without the dielectric. This formula indicates that the capacitance increases by a factor of k when a dielectric material is present. Therefore, option A) Increases by k accurately describes the effect of a dielectric on the capacitance.

This option suggests that the presence of a dielectric material has no effect on the capacitance of the capacitor. However, this is not accurate. The presence of a dielectric material between the plates of a capacitor increases the capacitance, as explained in option A). Therefore, option B) Has no effect is incorrect.

This option suggests that the capacitance of the capacitor decreases by a factor of k when a dielectric material is inserted. However, this is not the case. The presence of a dielectric material increases the capacitance by a factor of k, as explained in option A). Therefore, option C) Decreases by k is incorrect.

This option suggests that the capacitance of the capacitor increases by half of the dielectric constant (k) when a dielectric material is inserted. However, this is not accurate. The presence of a dielectric material increases the capacitance by the full value of the dielectric constant (k), not by half of it. Therefore, option D) Increases by k/2 is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Introducing a dielectric in a capacitor causes a decrease in the electric field, and hence the voltage. The charge stored on the plate remains unchanged.As C = Q/V,decreasing V causes an increase in capacitance.The capacitance of a parallel plate capacitor is given by:capacitance C = (area of plate) (permittivity of free space) / (distance between the plates) = Aεo/d ..........(1)Introducing the dielectric, the capacitance becomes:capacitance Cm = (area of plate) (permittivity of medium) / (distance between the plates) = Aεm/d .......... (2)The relative permittivity is given by:k = εm/εoor εm = kεoPutting (εm = kεo ) in equation 2:Cm = Akεo/d = Aεo/d x kCm = C x kHence, the introduction of dielectric causes the capacitance to increase by k times.

questions.text: Energy stored in a capacitor is given by:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option suggests that the energy stored in a capacitor is given by QV/2 which is a correct formula.
This option suggests that the energy stored in a capacitor is given by CV²/2 which is a correct formula.
This option suggests that the energy stored in a capacitor is given by Q²/2C which is a correct formula.
This option suggests that all of the above formulas are correct. This option is the correct choice since options A, B, and C indeed give correct formulas for energy stored in a capacitor.

questions.explanation: In a capacitor, charge +Q and -Q is stored on one plate each. The potential difference between the plates is V, and the average potential difference during the charging process is V/2. Thus, the energy stored in a capacitor is:U = QV/2 ............(1)putting Q = CV in (1):U = CV2/2putting V = Q/C in (1)U = Q2/2C

questions.text: What will be the effect on the capacitance of a capacitor if area of each plate is doubled while separation between the plates is halved?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Capacitance remains the same: This would only happen if the changes had no impact or cancelled each other out, which they don't.
Capacitance becomes double: This only considers the effect of one change (either area or separation) but not both.
The initial doubling from the area change is multiplied by the second doubling from the separation change:2C * 2 = 4CTherefore, the correct answer is:* Capacitance becomes four times
Capacitance reduces to half: This implies an inverse relationship with both area and separation, which is incorrect. The area increase actually increases capacitance.

questions.explanation: The formula for capacitance is𝐶=ε𝐴 / 𝑑where,A is the area of the plate, d is the distance between the two parallel plates.If area is doubled, it becomes 2Aif distance is halved it becomes d/2So the new capacitance will be,𝐶2=ε2𝐴÷ 𝑑/2 = 4ε𝐴/𝑑Since 𝐶1 = ε𝐴/𝑑𝐶2= 4𝐶1Hence capacitance will become 4 times.

questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: We know when capacitors are in series ,charge through them is same So C and D options are obviously wrongAs voltage given is equivalent Voltage So we have to find equivalent capacitance to find charge 1/Ceq = 1/C1+ 1/ C21/Ceq = 1/ 12 + 1/ 241/Ceq = 1+2 / 24Ceq = 24 / 3 = 8 F Now ,Q= CVQ=8×36Q=288CSo A option is right.
This option is incorrect based on calculations.
This is wrong because charge on both capacitors must be same when they are connected in series.
This is wrong because charge on both capacitors must be same when they are connected in series.

questions.explanation: In series, charge remains same. This cancels option C and D.To calculate Total capacitance:1/C = 1/12 + 1/24 C=8FC=Q/V8 x 36= 288COption A is therefore the correct answer.

questions.text: Electric Potential which is measured with respect to the zero potential is called:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Absolute electric potential is the amount of potential energy needed to move a unit positive charge from infinity to a specific point within the field. The electric potential at infinity is assumed to be zero.
Change in kinetic energy is the difference between the final and initial kinetic energy.

Change in gravitational potential energy, ΔP.Eg, is ΔP.Eg = mgh, with h being the increase in height and g the acceleration due to gravity. The gravitational potential energy of an object near the Earth's surface is due to its position in the mass-Earth system.
Electric intensity at a point is the force on a unit positive charge at that point.

questions.explanation: Absolute electric potential is the amount of potential energy needed to move a unit positive charge from infinity to a specific point within the field. The electric potential at infinity is assumed to be zero.

questions.text: The flux through a surface will be zero when the angle between E and △A is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Flux through an area will be zero when both electric field and area or surface are perpendicular to each other.
Option B (60°) is incorrect because a non-zero flux can still occur if the electric field and the surface normal have a component along each other.
Option C (30°) is incorrect because even though the angle is acute, a non-zero flux can still occur if the electric field and the surface normal have a component along each other.
Option D (0°) is incorrect because a non-zero flux is expected when the electric field and the surface normal are parallel, resulting in maximum flux.

questions.explanation: As; Ø=EA cosθAnd cos 90° = 0

questions.text: The device used to store a charge is called:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Electric box protects the connections, which usually contain vulnerable points such as wire splices, from environmental conditions and accidental contact.
A capacitor is a device used to store an electric charge, consisting of one or more pairs of conductors separated by an insulator.
An inductor is a passive two-terminal electrical component that stores energy in a magnetic field when electric current flows through it.
A transistor is a semiconductor device used to amplify or switch electrical signals and power.

questions.explanation: It is the function of capacitors to store charge.

questions.text: The electric flux through the surface of the sphere is directly proportional to the:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Electric flux is directly proportional to the surface area of the sphere. This is because the electric field lines pass through the surface of the sphere and the number of field lines passing through a given area is proportional to that area. As the surface area increases, the number of field lines passing through it also increases, leading to a higher electric flux.this is partially correct.

Electric flux is not directly proportional to the radius of the sphere. While the radius does affect the magnitude of the electric field at different points on the sphere's surface, it is not the primary factor determining the electric flux through the surface.

options.text: Charge at the centre of the sphere
options.explanation: According to Guass' Law flux through a surface is proportional to the charge enclosed at its center.

The electric flux through a closed surface is determined by the net charge enclosed within that surface, regardless of the nature (positive or negative) of the charge. The nature of charge does not directly affect the proportionality of electric flux to any of the given factors.

questions.explanation: According to Guass' Law flux through a surface is proportional to the charge enclosed at its center.

options.explanation: This is correct option because for capacitors in series, the total capacitance can be found by adding the reciprocals of the individual capacitances, and taking the reciprocal of the sum.

For capacitors in parallel in a circuit, the input leads of all capacitors are connected together, and the output leads of all the capacitors are connected together. The equivalent capacitance for capacitors connected in parallel is given by the formula Ceq=C1+C2+... +Cn.
This option is incorrect because the formula given is for series not for parallel.
This option is incorrect because option A is correct.

questions.explanation: Correct option is A.For capacitors in series, the total capacitance can be found by adding the reciprocals of the individual capacitances, and taking the reciprocal of the sum.

questions.text: If we double the separation between two charges, then coulomb's force will become?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as per formula
Incorrect as per formula
Incorrect as per formula

According to Coloumbs law, F=kq1q2/d² where F is the force, k=1/4πε0 , q1 and q2 are the charges and d is the distance of separation of charges.So F is inversely proportional to 1/d²If the separation is doubled, d becomes 2dthen the force decreases by 4 times and becomes F/4.

questions.explanation: The size of the force varies inversely as the square of the distance between the two charges. Therefore, if the distance between the two charges is doubled, the attraction or repulsion becomes weaker, decreasing to one-fourth of the original value.

questions.text: Statement and argument:Statement: Should the public sector undertakings be allowed to adopt a hire-and-fire policy?Arguments:(I) Yes, as this will help the public sector undertakings to get rid of non-performing employees and will also help to reward the performing employees.(II) No, the management may not be able to implement the policy in an unbiased manner and the employees will suffer due to the high-handedness of the management.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Hire and fire policy' implies 'taking up the performing employees and discarding the non-performing ones'. Clearly, such a policy would stand out to encourage employees to work hard and devotedly to retain their jobs and thus enhance productivity and profitability of the organizations. Argument II seems to be vague in the light of this.

This option is incorrect because both arguments have valid points, and it is not evident that one argument is significantly stronger than the other. Argument II raises concerns about potential management biases in implementing the policy.

options.text: Both arguments I and II are strong
options.explanation: Both arguments have valid points, and it is not evident that one argument is significantly stronger than the other. Argument I is strong because it highlights a practical benefit of adopting a hire-and-fire policy: removing non-performing employees can increase efficiency, and rewarding performing employees can improve motivation. This argument is logical and directly supports the statement. Argument II is also strong because it presents a real and reasonable concern. In many public sector undertakings, management may misuse such policies due to bias or corruption. This may lead to injustice and insecurity among employees. This argument is also logical and relevant.

options.text: Neither argument I nor II is strong
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because both arguments have reasonable points, even though they also have some flaws. It is not accurate to dismiss both arguments entirely.

questions.explanation: Both arguments have valid points, and it is not evident that one argument is significantly stronger than the other. Argument I is strong because it highlights a practical benefit of adopting a hire-and-fire policy: removing non-performing employees can increase efficiency, and rewarding performing employees can improve motivation. This argument is logical and directly supports the statement. Argument II is also strong because it presents a real and reasonable concern. In many public sector undertakings, management may misuse such policies due to bias or corruption. This may lead to injustice and insecurity among employees. This argument is also logical and relevant.

questions.text: Statement and Argument Statement: Should admission to the professional degrees in Pakistan be given only on merit without any concession to any particular group of students? Arguments:(I) Yes. This will improve the quality of the professional institutes and degrees as they will be able to complete the degree successfully. (II) No. This will keep a large number of socially and economically backward students out of the reach of professional institutes and degrees.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Argument II is also very strong.

options.text: If only argument II is strong
options.explanation: Argument I is also strong.

options.text: If both I and II are strong.
options.explanation: According to the statement I only all the hardworking students will be able to avail the admission in the professional degree hence there will be almost 100% course completion and success rate. But statement II also has a strong argument i.e. that if no concessions are made and admissions are solely on the base of merit then the socially and economically backward students won't be able to enroll in these professional degrees. So option C is correct.

options.text: If neither I nor II is strong
options.explanation: Both arguments are very strong.

questions.explanation: According to the statement I only all the hardworking students will be able to avail the admission in the professional degree hence there will be almost 100% course completion and success rate. But statement II also has a strong argument i.e. that if no concessions are made and admissions are solely on the base of merit then the socially and economically backward students won't be able to enroll in these professional degrees. So option C is correct.
